channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Author Correction: Solution-sheared supramolecular oligomers with enhanced thermal resistance in interfacial adhesion and bulk cohesion
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69146-0

Author Correction: Solution-sheared supramolecular oligomers with enhanced thermal resistance in interfacial adhesion and bulk cohesion
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Solution-sheared supramolecular oligomers with enhanced thermal resistance in interfacial adhesion and bulk cohesion
item.dc:creator: Gang Lu
Rui Ma
Yuanyuan Zhao
Dianyu Wang
Wentao Shang
Huaguo Chen
Shahid Ali Khan
Ming Li
Eduardo Saiz

item.title: A self-regulated photothermal anti-/deicing film for all-season applications
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69494-x

A self-regulated photothermal superhydrophobic film with strong solar modulation, energy storage, and durability is reported, switching between black and white as temperature changes, enabling winter anti-/deicing and summer overheating prevention.
item.dc:title: A self-regulated photothermal anti-/deicing film for all-season applications
item.dc:creator: Jiayu Du
Wenqi Wang
Yang Fu
Xin Li
Jie Tan
Hao Li
Xu Chen
Fuqiang Chu
Qi Min
Chi Yan Tso

item.title: Entropy-mediated solidification stabilizes and enhances energetic release in amorphous energetic materials
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69256-9

This work develops a strategy to construct a stable amorphous energetic material with self-healing capability via entropy-mediated solidification. The material exhibits enhanced structural stability, improved energy release, and low sensitivity, offering insights for designing high-performance energetic materials.
item.dc:title: Entropy-mediated solidification stabilizes and enhances energetic release in amorphous energetic materials
item.dc:creator: Xu Zhou
Zhiqiang Wang
Hui Huang
Yu Liu
Shichun Li
Wen Qian
Shiliang Huang
Jinjiang Xu
